message:
style(wa-3mfv9): apply rustfmt canonical layout to char-props skip closures

Purely mechanical rustfmt pass on the wa-3mfv9 regression guard test
file landed in 917be29a. The two `path.ancestors().any(|a| { ... })`
blocks that skip the `frankenterm/char-props/codegen/` tree (once in
`is_supported_rust_file`, once in `collect_workspace_cargo_manifests`)
get collapsed from the braced closure form to the idiomatic chained
form:

    path
        .ancestors()
        .any(|a| a.file_name().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) == Some("char-props"))

No logic, no predicates, no control flow changed — the closure body,
comparison target, and `return false` / `continue` outcomes are
byte-identical; only whitespace and `{ }` framing moved. `cargo fmt`
now reports clean on this file, which matters because the guard
itself is a `cargo test` target and any lint/fmt drift on it becomes
a meta-regression in the very invariant the file exists to pin.
